purpose: Lightweight routing + output-contract fixtures for website-scraping. Specs, not executed live in CI; validated by tests/test_routing_fixtures.py. Complements evals/evals.json (which holds runnable end-to-end scraping tasks) by capturing routing boundaries and the required output contract.

fixtures.request: Pull every product on the first page of example-shop.com — name, price, and product URL — into a file I can load later.

expected_routing.reason: Turning one or more URLs into clean structured records is the core website-scraping job.

fixtures.request: Get all the quotes, authors, and tags from a JavaScript-rendered listing page and save them as records.

expected_routing.reason: Client-rendered extraction (escalate to in-page JSON or Playwright) is still website-scraping.

fixtures.request: Research the background and any adverse media on Acme Corp before we sign a contract.

expected_routing.reason: Background/vetting of a named company is entity-research, not generic site scraping.

fixtures.request: I have a CSV of 200 people — add each person's current employer and LinkedIn URL.

expected_routing.reason: Bulk person/company enrichment via a licensed data provider is people-enrichment, not DIY scraping (and LinkedIn scraping is out of scope).
